samples.xml_description: Implement a 16-bit divider, the dividend is 16-bit and the divider is 8-bit in combinational logic. Extract the higher bits of the dividend, matching the bit width of the divisor. Compare these bits with the divisor: if the dividend bits are greater, set the quotient to 1, otherwise set it to 0, and use the difference as the remainder. Concatenate the remainder with the highest remaining 1-bit of the dividend, and repeat the process until all dividend bits are processed. 16-bit dividend. 8-bit divisor. 16-bit quotient. 16-bit remainder. None. shares a common bus for the FSM states and 1-bit internal constants. // Use always block to implement the functionality of the divider. // always block is triggered when the inputs A or B change. always @(A | B) // Use the ternary operator to select the larger value between the higher bits of A and B for the dividend. // Assign the selected value to the register a_reg.   a_reg = (a_reg >= b_reg) ? a_reg - b_reg : a_reg + b_reg; // Assign the value of B to the register b_reg.   b_reg = b_reg; // The if statement checks if the dividend is greater than or equal to the divisor. // If it is, set the quotient to 1 and the remainder to the difference, otherwise set the quotient to 0 and the remainder to the dividend. if (a_reg >= b_reg)   quotient = 1;   remainder = a_reg - b_reg; else   quotient = 0;   remainder = a_reg; // Concatenate the remainder with the quotient to form the output result. result = {remainder, quotient}; // Concatenate the remainder with the quotient to form the output odd. odd = {remainder, quotient}; }
